sql: SELECT article_title FROM articles WHERE article_id IN (SELECT article_id_cited FROM citing_cited GROUP BY article_id_cited HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT MAX(citation_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS citation_count FROM citing_cited GROUP BY article_id_cited)))
template: SELECT article_title FROM articles WHERE article_id IN (SELECT article_id_cited FROM citing_cited GROUP BY article_id_cited HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT MAX(citation_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS citation_count FROM citing_cited GROUP BY article_id_cited)))
question: Which article has been cited the most times according to the records?
answer: AN IMAGE IS WORTH 16x16 WORDS: TRANSFORMERS FOR IMAGE RECOGNITION AT SCALE, SOME ASPECTS OF THE SEQUENTIAL DESIGN OF EXPERIMENTS, Probabilistic Model for Code with Decision Trees, An Analysis of Active Learning Strategies for Sequence Labeling Tasks, Bayesian-Optimal Design via Interacting Particle Systems, Game-Theoretic Statistics and Safe Anytime-Valid Inference, Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
label: training

sql: SELECT MIN(title_word_count) FROM articles WHERE reference_count / 5 = title_word_count;
template: SELECT MIN(title_word_count) FROM articles WHERE reference_count / 5 = title_word_count;
question: What is the minimum number of words in the title of articles where the number of references divided by five equals the number of words in the title?
label: training

sql: SELECT article_title FROM articles WHERE title_word_count <> 6 ORDER BY title_word_count;
template: SELECT article_title FROM articles WHERE title_word_count <> {title-word-count} ORDER BY title_word_count;
